cling: noun. Fruit (especially peach) whose flesh adheres strongly to the pit.
clog: noun. A type of shoe with an inflexible, often wooden sole sometimes with an open heel.
clop: noun. The sound of a horse 's shod hoof striking the ground.
cluing: verb. present participle and gerund of clue
clung: verb. simple past and past participle of cling
cog: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chong.
coign: noun. A projecting corner or angle; a cornerstone.
coil: noun. Something wound in the form of a helix or spiral.
coin: noun. A piece of currency, usually metallic and in the shape of a disc, but sometimes polygonal, or with a hole in the middle.
col: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Columbia-Wenatchi.
con: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cofán.
cop: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Coptic.
coping: noun. The top layer of a brick wall, especially one that slopes in order to throw off water.
cup: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cupeño.
gluon: noun. A massless gauge boson that binds quarks together to form baryons, mesons and other hadrons and is associated with the strong nuclear
gnu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gnau.
gul: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gullah.
gun: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mbya Guarani.
icon: noun. An image, symbol, picture, portrait, or other representation, usually as an object of religious devotion.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
lin: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Lingala.
ling: noun. Any of various codlike saltwater fish often used as food by humans, those of the genus Molva, in the family Lotidae.
lingo: noun. Language, especially language peculiar to a particular group, field, or region; jargon or a dialect.
loin: noun. The part of the body (of humans and quadrupeds) at each side of the backbone, between the ribs and hips.
long: adjective. Having much distance in space from one end to the other.
lop: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lopa.
loping: verb. present participle and gerund of lope
loup: noun. A mass or ball of iron in a pasty condition, beaten by the trip hammer or rolls.
louping: noun. Alternative form of louping ill.
lug: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Luganda.
nil: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nila.
nip: verb. To catch and enclose or compress tightly between two surfaces, or points which are brought together or closed; to pinch; to close in
oilcup: noun. A device that dispenses oil for lubrication in an engine, etc., without the need for a human operator.
oping: verb. present participle and gerund of ope
pig: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pisabo.
pin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Piame.
ping: noun. A high-pitched, short and somewhat sharp sound.
pingo: noun. A conical mound of earth with an ice core caused by permafrost uplift, particularly if lasting more than a year.
pion: noun. Any of three semistable mesons, having positive, negative or neutral charge, composed of up and down quarks / antiquarks.
piu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pintupi-Luritja.
pong: noun. A stench, a bad smell.
pug: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Phuie.
pul: noun. A subdivision of currency, equal to one hundredth of an Afghan afghani.
puli: noun. One of a breed of Hungarian sheepdog with a distinctive thick, corded coat.
puling: verb. present participle and gerund of pule
pun: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Pubian.
pung: noun. A low box -like sleigh designed to be pulled by one horse.
unclip: verb. To release something by removing a clip.
unclog: verb. To remove a blockage from.
